chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Hello friends, welcome to the part 4 of the computer training class.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Today we will learn the features of Windows desktop which are used daily in office and corporate environment.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: In this class, you will learn how to open a program and software properly, how to manage Windows efficiently,

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Snap Assist for multitasking, Desktop shortcut and folder management, Desktop icons and personalization.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Similarly, we will perform many more tasks. All these things will help you a lot in job, interview and practical office work.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So let's start today's class without wasting time.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: First of all, let's learn what are the correct ways to open a folder or program like Google Chrome, AutoCAD or MS Excel.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: There are many ways to open a program, but these three are used the most.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: 1. Desktop shortcut. If you have made a program shortcut on the desktop, then take the mouse cursor to that icon and double-click it.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Or right-click on the icon and click on the open option given at the top.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Let's close it now.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: 2. Taskbar. This is the easiest way. If there is a program in your taskbar, then you just have to click on it once and the program will open.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: 3. Start Menu. This is a professional way. It is considered the fastest way to work in the office.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Just press the Windows key on the keyboard, type the program's name, like Excel and press Enter.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: After the program opens, the screen that comes in front of you is called the program window.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: This is the title bar of the window. There are three main buttons on the top right of any window.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: This is the minimize button. By clicking on it, the window is hidden and goes to the taskbar.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: To bring it back, click on the same program icon in the taskbar again.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: This is the maximize button. With this button, you can full screen the window.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: When the window is full screen, this button becomes restore down. By clicking on it, the window comes to its previous small size.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If you want to give the window the size of your choice, then take the cursor to any corner of the window.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: When the shape of the cursor changes, then press and hold the left click and drag the mouse to make its size smaller or larger.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: This red button is to turn off the program. Its keyboard shortcut is also Alt F4, with which you can close any active window immediately.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Multitasking is the most important thing in corporate life.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Suppose you have to see data from Chrome on one side and type in Word on the other side.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: For this, we use Snap Assist or Split Screen. Let's learn how to do this.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Take the cursor on the title bar and press and hold the left click. Now drag the window to the left or right corner of the screen and leave it.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Select your other program like Chrome from the other side.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: This will split your screen into two equal parts and you will be able to do two things at a time.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: It also has a shortcut. Press the Windows key plus left or right arrow on the keyboard.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Press the arrow. This will split your screen into two equal parts.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: It is most important to save time while working in a corporate office.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If your necessary files and software are in front, then work is done quickly.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Desktop shortcuts are very useful for this.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: There are many ways to make a desktop shortcut for a program.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: First, click on the Start button and find the software you want to bring to the desktop.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now right click on the software, go to More, click on Open File Location.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now right click on the software icon in the window that will open.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Select Send To and click on Desktop Create Shortcut.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: The second easiest way to make a shortcut is to click on the Start button, find the software,

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: press and hold the left mouse button on it and drag it to the desktop.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Let's see another way to make a shortcut for a folder or file.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If your file is hidden in a drive, then right click on the desktop, go to New, click on Shortcut.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now press the Browse button and select the file or folder you want to make a shortcut.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Then click on Next and finish.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So friends, this way you can make a desktop shortcut for any program, file or folder.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: How to pin a program in the taskbar, we have learned in the last video.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Windows has some default icons like This PC, Recycle Bin and Control Panel.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Sometimes they disappear from the desktop or we have to remove them.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Let's learn how to manage them.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Click on the Window button, go to Settings, then click on Personalize.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Or right click on the desktop and click on Personalize from the bottom.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now in the open Settings window, click on the left side theme,

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: now scroll down a little on the right side and click on Desktop Icon Settings.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: A small window will open in front of you.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Here you can tick the checkbox of the icons you want on the desktop,

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: those who want to remove them, untick them.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Then click on OK and close the settings.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now you will be able to see that your selected icons are visible on the desktop.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: To manage office work professionally, it is most important that your files are arranged in different folders.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: This saves you time in finding any file later.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Let's see how to make a folder on the desktop.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Right click on the desktop from any empty space, go to the New option given below and then click on Folder.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now you will see that a folder has come on the screen, which has been automatically named as New Folder by Windows.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now give it a professional name like Project 2026 or Daily Reports and press Enter on the keyboard.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Your folder is ready.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: The shortcut to create a folder is Ctrl Shift N.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: And if you want to rename a folder, then take the mouse cursor over that folder,

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: click on it once, that is, select it.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now you can press F2 on the keyboard to rename.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: You must have seen that people keep clicking on the desktop and refreshing.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Maybe they believe that this makes the computer faster, but this is not true.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: The refresh button only updates or redraws the desktop screen.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Sometimes when you change the name of a file or make a new folder and it is not visible on the screen immediately,

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: then refreshing will update it.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: But nowadays Windows has auto refresh feature, so you do not need to do this again and again.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If your PC is running slow, then refreshing it again and again will not increase its speed.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Before moving forward in the video, if you like this video, then like the video.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If you are not on the channel, then subscribe to the channel.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So let's move forward in the video.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Let's learn how to manage and arrange desktop icons according to your needs and likes.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: First of all, right click on the empty space of the desktop.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: The pop-up menu will open.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Take the cursor on the top view option.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Here you will get three main options.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Large icons, if you want to see big icons, then choose this.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Medium icons or default size, which most people use.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Small icons, if you have a lot of files on your desktop, then choosing small icons will take up less space.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Friends, there is also a shortcut.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Go to the desktop and press the control button on the keyboard and rotate the scroll wheel of the mouse up and down.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: You can also increase the size of the icon immediately.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Often we leave the files here and there, which makes the desktop look dirty.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: We can also set these desktop icons automatically.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: For this, right click on the desktop, go to the view, auto arrange icons.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: As soon as you click on it, windows will automatically set all the icons on the left side of the screen.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: After this, you will not be able to manually place the icons anywhere in the middle.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: You can just drag them back and forth.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Align icons to grid.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: By keeping it on, the icons remain in a straight line and a proper grid.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So that they do not climb on top of each other and the desktop looks clean.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Sometimes we need a clean desktop during a meeting or presentation.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So desktop icons.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If you click on it and uncheck it, all your icons will disappear.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Don't be afraid, your files have not been deleted.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Just by clicking on this option again, they will start to appear again.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: In this way, we can use the view menu to manage the desktop icons as per our needs.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Let's learn now how to change the theme and wallpaper of your desktop.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So that your workspace looks even more attractive.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: For this, go to personalize.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: In the same way, right click on the desktop, go to personalize.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now go to the themes option.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Here you will see many themes under the change theme.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Click on your favorite theme.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: As soon as the theme changes, not only the wallpaper but also the color of the taskbar and text also changes.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Like the blue one was before, it has now changed according to the theme.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If you don't like the themes given here, then you can click on the browse theme or get more themes in Microsoft store.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: You can download thousands of new themes.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If you want to change the wallpaper, then go to personalize and click on the background.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Here select a picture and select one from the image given below.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: To put your favorite photo, click on the browse button.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Select any of your favorite photos from your computer and choose a picture.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Your wallpaper has been updated.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now let's talk about the screen saver.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: When you take a break in the office, your name is shown on the screen.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So it looks very professional here.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Let's see how this will happen.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Right click on the desktop and go to personalize.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now click on the lock screen on the left.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Scroll down and click on the screen saver setting.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now select 3D text from the drop-down menu under the screen saver in the small window that will open.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: By default, the name of windows is written here.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Now if you want to write your name, click on the setting for this.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Type your name in the custom text box.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Here you can also change the way the text is rotated.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Like I do a spin slide from here and click on OK.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Click on the preview button and see how it looks.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: The time written in the weight box below, like 1 minute, means that if you do not touch the keyboard or mouse for 1 minute, then the screen saver will automatically start.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: You can do it as per your choice.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: So friends, in today's class, we learned how to manage the Windows desktop professionally.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: We learned how to open the program, multitasking from Windows Management and SnapAssist, desktop shortcuts and folder management, changing themes and wallpapers, setting screen savers.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If you bring all these things in practice every day, then believe me, your work will be fast, clean and professional.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: If you found this class useful, then do practice it.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: And in the next class, we will learn more important computer skills.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: Like the video, if you are new to the channel, then subscribe to the channel and share it with your friends who want to learn computers.

chunks.title: Class
chunks.text: See you in the next video, till then keep practicing.
